items.verse: وَأُتْبِعُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعْنَةًۭ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ عَادًۭا كَفَرُوا۟ رَبَّهُمْ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًۭا لِّعَادٍۢ قَوْمِ هُودٍۢ
items.verseEn: And they were [therefore] followed in this world with a curse and [as well] on the Day of Resurrection. Unquestionably, ʿAad denied their Lord; then away with ʿAad, the people of Hūd.

items.verse: ۞ وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ هُوَ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيهَا فَٱسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى قَرِيبٌۭ مُّجِيبٌۭ
items.verseEn: And to Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. He has produced you from the earth and settled you in it, so ask forgiveness of Him and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive."

items.verse: كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًۭا لِّمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ
items.verseEn: As if they had never prospered therein. Then, away with Madyan as Thamūd was taken away.

items.verse: أَهُمْ خَيْرٌ أَمْ قَوْمُ تُبَّعٍۢ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ
items.verseEn: Are they better or the people of Tubbaʿ and those before them? We destroyed them, [for] indeed, they were criminals.

categories.name: المُقْسَماتُ الطَّبِيعِيَّةُ فِي القُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Natural Oath Subjects in the Quran

items.verseEn: And [by] the sky and He who constructed it
And [by] the earth and He who spread it
And [by] the night when it covers with darkness,
And [by] the day when it appears

categories.name: طَلَبَاتُ مُوسَى الْأَرْبَعَةُ فِي سُورَةِ طه
categories.nameEn: Musa's Four Requests in Surah Ta-Ha

items.verseEn: [Moses] said, "My Lord, expand [i.e., relax] for me my breast [with assurance]
And ease for me my task
And untie the knot from my tongue
And appoint for me a minister [i.e., assistant] from my family -

categories.name: مُفْرَدَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ ذِي ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ
categories.nameEn: Key Elements from the Story of Dhul-Qarnayn

items.verse: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍۢ لَّمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُم مِّن دُونِهَا سِتْرًۭا
items.verseEn: Until, when he came to the rising of the sun [i.e., the east], he found it rising on a people for whom We had not made against it any shield.

items.verse: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّدَّيْنِ وَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمَا قَوْمًۭا لَّا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ قَوْلًۭا
items.verseEn: Until, when he reached [a pass] between two mountains, he found beside them a people who could hardly understand [his] speech.

items.verse: فَمَا ٱسْطَـٰعُوٓا۟ أَن يَظْهَرُوهُ وَمَا ٱسْتَطَـٰعُوا۟ لَهُۥ نَقْبًۭا
items.verseEn: So they [i.e., Gog and Magog] were unable to pass over it, nor were they able [to effect] in it any penetration.

harf.hint: Authority or clear proof granted by God to His messengers
harf.arabicVerse: ۞ قَالَتْ رُسُلُهُمْ أَفِى ٱللَّهِ شَكٌّۭ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِيَغْفِرَ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرَكُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍۢ مُّسَمًّۭى ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُنَا تُرِيدُونَ أَن تَصُدُّونَا عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا فَأْتُونَا بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
harf.verse: 14:10 - Their messengers said, "Can there be doubt about Allāh, Creator of the heavens and earth? He invites you that He may forgive you of your sins, and He delays you [i.e., your death] for a specified term." They said, "You are not but men like us who wish to avert us from what our fathers were worshipping. So bring us a clear authority [i.e., evidence]."

deduction.title: The Sacred Absence
deduction.intro: Beyond the dunes of time, there lies a tale of unwavering devotion and a trust so heavy that the soul carrying it could have broken. In a moment when the people turned toward folly and gold, one figure stood firm—calling them back, pleading for patience, and bearing the weight of a sacred promise. This is the story of one who was chosen as a helper, a vizier, and a guardian of faith during a sacred absence. Can you uncover the identity of this steadfast soul?
clues: I share the blood of a great prophet who was chosen to speak with Allah directly upon a sacred mount.
My sibling once pleaded with Allah for assistance in speaking clearly to a great oppressor.
I was chosen by Allah to stand beside my sibling as a helper and vizier.
Together we faced a tyrannical ruler who demanded to be called the highest of gods.
While my sibling communed with Allah upon the mountain, our people fashioned for themselves an idol from their gold.
I am the brother of Moses, appointed by Allah as his vizier, mentioned by name in the Quran.
deduction.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلْنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ وَزِيرًۭا
deduction.verse: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant. (25:35)

scramble.hint: This verse describes the reaction of disbelievers to a sign from Allah, showing how they reject truth out of arrogance.
scramble.verseEn: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken [for Himself] a son."
